_comment: Drives the public docs site at houwert.dev/conductor/docs. Each entry pulls a markdown file from this directory, parses it, and publishes it to the marketing site at build time. To expose a new doc, add it here. Categories appear in declaration order; pages within a category appear in declaration order too.

pages.title: Getting started
pages.summary: Install Conductor, point it at a simulator or emulator, and run your first command in under a minute.

pages.title: Concepts
pages.summary: Drivers, sessions, devices, the daemon, and how Conductor stays out of the way of your AI agent.

pages.title: Command catalogue
pages.summary: Every command grouped by purpose — app lifecycle, interaction, inspection, assertions, devices, flows, and web.

pages.title: Flows
pages.summary: Run Maestro-compatible YAML flow files. Single-file, inline, and parallel-across-devices execution.

pages.title: Web testing
pages.summary: The same commands you use on iOS and Android, driving Playwright-managed Chromium, Firefox, or WebKit instead.

pages.title: Experimental
pages.summary: RN debugger, network inspection, and profiling. Powerful but depend on React Native runtime internals — expect occasional drift between RN versions.

pages.title: Privacy
pages.summary: Conductor is a pure CLI. No telemetry, no analytics, no phone-home — what you run on your machine stays on your machine.
